name: taskforge-vscode
description: Render the TaskForge inside VS Code, with a one-click Start button when the server isn't running.

viewsWelcome.contents:
The TaskForge server isn't running yet.
[▶ Start TaskForge](command:taskforge.start)
Once it's up, your tasks appear here — click one to open it in an editor tab.

No tasks yet. Import a change to create one, then refresh.
[↻ Refresh](command:taskforge.refreshTasks)

taskforge.rootPath.description: Absolute path to the TaskForge repo (the folder containing vite.config.mjs). Leave blank to auto-detect from open workspace folders, then fall back to the extension's own location.

taskforge.host.description: Host used to build the TaskForge URL rendered in the webview (e.g. 127.0.0.1 or taskforge).

enumDescriptions: Reopen the page's primary (root) chat in the chat panel with the question pre-filled — you press Enter. Full context, human-in-the-loop.
Headlessly resume the root chat (claude -p --resume) so it answers with zero keystrokes. Full context, but appends autonomous turns to that session.
Headlessly run a separate per-page reviewer session. Zero-touch and non-invasive, but starts context-light.
Never auto-trigger a reply — just post the question.
taskforge.respondMode.description: What happens when you post a question in a TaskForge thread.

taskforge.resumeCommand.description: Terminal command used to reopen a non-Claude linked chat. Placeholders: ${sessionId}, ${cwd}, ${tool}.

taskforge.claudeCliPath.description: Path to the Claude CLI used by the headless respond modes (the VS Code extension's bundled copy is not on PATH; install the standalone CLI or set an absolute path).
